example_prompt: Validate a currency code and get its decimal_places to format amounts correctly before calling create_payment.

name.description: Full currency name, e.g. "Central African CFA franc".

faction.description: Number of decimal places (e.g. 0 for XAF, 2 for USD). Field name is 'faction', not 'decimal_places'.

gotchas: This endpoint returns ALL world currencies (ISO 4217 global list), NOT only currencies accepted by Notch Pay — it is a reference/validation endpoint. The actual currency used across all Notch Pay channels is XAF regardless of the customer's country.
Use the 'faction' field to format amounts correctly — XAF has faction: 0, so amounts must be integers. Sending 1000.50 for XAF will cause a validation error.
Use the currency code (not the name) when specifying currency in payment or channel API calls; the name field is for display purposes only.
The Authorization header uses your public key with no 'Bearer ' prefix.
